description: Golden identifier set the automated pipeline should retrieve/resolve for the Case 10 reference case (the FINAL case of the 10-case slate): an EHR-based (± wearable/bed-sensor) predictive decision-support algorithm that flags hospitalized adults at high risk of an in-hospital fall and prompts fall-prevention precautions. Companion to the HRV/DR/warfarin/sepsis/AFib/melanoma/depression/pneumonia/pembrolizumab goldens. REGULATORY-NULL for the system under evaluation: there is NO FDA product code, De Novo, 510(k), or PMA for a predictive EHR-based inpatient fall-RISK CDS algorithm. The scored FDA landscape is the ADJACENT fall-related HARDWARE only: Class II Wearable Fall Injury Prevention Device (SEC, via De Novo DEN240021 = Active Protective Tango Belt) + four Class I alarm/sensor/monitor codes (PJO/PJP/KMI/SBO, all 21 CFR 880.2400) + two cleared hardware 510(k)s (K141877 Leaf Patient Monitoring KMI; K233096 PressureAlert SBO). No PMA exists for SEC/PJO/SBO (verified against the LWP positive control -> 2,368 PMA records, so the empty result is a genuine null, not a query error); no NDA/biologic. Use in the ablation harness: score recall = golden ids present in the bundle's records (FDA codes / K / DEN EXACT; literature as a >=K resolvable-ID floor per COMPARISON.md Lesson 2), precision = on-topic rate. Every id in 'required' resolved live 2026-07-09 (PMIDs via E-utils esummary, DOIs via Crossref works, product codes via openFDA device/classification, DEN via openFDA device/510k k_number field, K via openFDA device/510k).
reference_case: Inpatient fall-risk prediction decision-support algorithm: analyzes routinely-collected EHR data (mobility/gait scores, culprit medications, prior-fall history, labs; optionally wearable/bed-sensor signals) to predict which hospitalized adults are at high risk of an in-hospital fall, deployed as a workflow alert to nursing teams. Reference standard = documented in-hospital fall events. A prognostic (risk-prediction) model validated in three stages (development+internal / temporal+geographic external / cluster-randomized or stepped-wedge clinical-impact), reported to TRIPOD+AI and appraised with PROBAST.
mesh_note: Condition authored condition-forward ('inpatient fall-risk prediction / hospitalized adults / in-hospital fall'), so Case-4 Finding A (mechanism-first phrasing zeroing retrieval) cannot bite. MeSH divergence on the '+hierarchy' axis is read empirically from the sweep, not asserted in advance ('Accidental Falls' is the relevant heading; whether the 5-slot _mesh_candidates cap admits the bare token is observed, not assumed).

match_policy.required_min_coverage: All ids in 'required' should be present for full recall. 'field_grounding' is the per-field breakdown following each source's role in the spec's SOURCE INVENTORY 'Grounded field(s)' columns. Fall-risk has NO scored 'ncts' category: every trial that informed the spec is design/enrollment CONTEXT only (registry enrollment magnitudes, not numeric benchmarks), so the 7 NCTs live in 'context_only_identifiers' and are NOT scored. It carries 'fda_product_codes' (SEC/PJO/PJP/KMI/SBO), 'fda_den_numbers' (DEN240021, the De Novo that created code SEC), and 'fda_k_numbers' (K141877/K233096). NO 'fda_pma_numbers' (genuine null, LWP-positive-control-verified) and NO 'fda_nda_numbers' (no drug/biologic product).
match_policy.resolution_pass: PMID -> E-utils esummary; DOI -> Crossref works; product_code -> openFDA device/classification; DEN (De Novo) -> openFDA device/510k (De Novo grants indexed with a DEN k_number - verified live: DEN240021 resolves via the 510k k_number field, matching the verify tool's DEN resolver; the device/denovo endpoint does NOT index it under denovo_number); K -> openFDA device/510k (k_number field). A required id that fails to resolve is a TEST FAILURE, not a silent drop.
match_policy.regulatory_null_for_the_system_under_eva: NULL: no FDA product code, De Novo, 510(k), or PMA exists for a predictive EHR-based inpatient fall-RISK CDS algorithm. The scored FDA ids are the ADJACENT fall-related HARDWARE landscape, not the predictive software itself. A genuinely novel predictive-CDS device would most plausibly enter via the De Novo route (no suitable predicate), IF determined to be a device at all after a CDS device/non-device determination with regulatory counsel.
match_policy.no_pma_positive_control: PMA searches on SEC/PJO/SBO each returned openFDA's genuine NOT_FOUND body. Verified individually against a positive control: product code LWP returns 2,368 PMA records, confirming the empty SEC/PJO/SBO result is a true zero rather than a query error. LWP is a CONTEXT-ONLY positive-control probe, NOT a scored fall-risk identifier.

context_only_identifiers.description: Identifiers that informed the spec as design/enrollment CONTEXT or as verification probes but are NOT scored. The harness and verify tool read ONLY the 'required' block, so nothing here affects recall/precision. Recorded for a complete audit trail.

ncts.reason: Fall TIPS interventional RCT (COMPLETED, n=10,264) - cited as impact-study design context (field 1) and realistic ward-level enrollment magnitude (field 5), NOT a numeric benchmark.
Fall TIPS generalizability/spread (observational, COMPLETED, n=37,231) - enrollment-magnitude context only (fields 1, 5).
Predictive Analytics & Computer Visualization for patient safety (interventional, COMPLETED, n=5,350) - impact-study design context (field 1).
Early fall-risk detection in inpatients (observational, COMPLETED, n=170) - design context (field 1).
FAIR AI-powered falls-risk tool, nurse trust/acceptance (interventional, NOT_YET_RECRUITING, n=60 est.) - real-world adoption / human-factors monitoring context (fields 1, 8).
Automated falls-risk screening & referral (observational, COMPLETED, n=10) - design context (field 1).
AI-driven nursing decision support (interventional, NOT_YET_RECRUITING, n=188 est.) - adoption / human-factors monitoring context (fields 1, 8).

fda_pma_positive_control.reason: Positive-control PMA probe ONLY: product code LWP returns 2,368 openFDA PMA records, proving the empty SEC/PJO/SBO PMA searches are a genuine null rather than a broken query. LWP is unrelated to fall-risk and must NEVER be scored.

regulatory_null_evidence.claim: No FDA marketing authorization (product code, De Novo, 510(k), or PMA) exists for a predictive EHR-based inpatient fall-RISK decision-support algorithm as of this session's openFDA queries. This is a genuine coverage gap, not a search miss: searches on predictive/fall-risk SOFTWARE product names returned zero device records. The fall-related FDA device records that DO exist are all HARDWARE (sensors/alarms/monitors and a wearable protective belt), not risk-prediction software.
regulatory_null_evidence.how_verified: openFDA device/classification, device/510k, and device/pma queried this session. Positive hardware codes SEC (Class II, via De Novo DEN240021)/PJO/PJP/KMI/SBO and the cleared hardware 510(k)s K141877 (Leaf, KMI)/K233096 (PressureAlert, SBO) all resolve and are scored in 'required'. PMA searches on SEC/PJO/SBO returned NOT_FOUND; the LWP positive control (2,368 PMA records) confirms the null is genuine. No De Novo or 510(k) for a fall-risk-prediction software algorithm was located.

1_study_design.note: Prognostic-model, three-stage validation reported to TRIPOD (25560714/25560730) and its AI extension TRIPOD+AI (38626948), appraised with PROBAST (30596876/30596875). Staged development->external->impact instantiated in the Cho program (30777849/34626168/37033322); unit-level cluster-randomized impact exemplified by the Fall TIPS toolkit trials (Dykes 21045097/33201236).

2_signal_validation.note: Validate the INPUTS: nursing fall-risk scales with imperfect measurement properties - Morse (2928815), Hendrich II (12624858), STRATIFY (9366729) and its meta-analysis (18829693) - need reliability checks; unstructured/nursing-record inputs carry real signal (NLP-of-nursing-records 32319959; standardized nursing terminologies 37507147). PROBAST applicability (30596875). Sensor-arm signal performance is regulated separately as HARDWARE (codes SEC/PJO/PJP/KMI/SBO), underscoring input validation as a distinct pre-condition.

3_performance_benchmarks.note: Report discrimination + calibration + clinical utility, not a single AUROC cutoff. Calibration as a hierarchy (Van Calster 31842878/26772608). Externally-validated inpatient fall comparators exist (Shim 36128798; Cho cross-site 30777849) but NO AUROC was retrieved from full text this session -> any comparator AUROC is a study-defined placeholder. Low-prevalence -> pair discrimination with precision/alert-burden per TRIPOD+AI (38626948).

4_ground_truth.note: Outcome = documented in-hospital fall with an injury-severity grade (Burns 31907532), ascertained from >=2 reconciled/adjudicated streams (Fall TIPS multi-source 33201236; STRIDE serious-fall-injury endpoint 32640131). Single-administrative-stream labels risk bias that propagates into performance estimates (Obermeyer 31649194).

5_sample_size.note: Compute sample size twice from published formulae: development (Riley 32188600), external validation (Riley/Snell 34915593; BMJ part-3 evaluation-sample-size 38253388). Because falls are rare, the binding constraint is the number of fall events (EPP); the specific N is a study-defined placeholder until the site's event fraction is fixed.

6_subgroup.note: Pre-specify subgroup discrimination AND calibration (a model can be calibrated overall yet miscalibrated in a subgroup - Van Calster 31842878) plus subgroup alert rates. TRIPOD+AI mandates fairness/subgroup reporting (38626948); PROBAST applicability covers transfer across subgroups (30596876/30596875); algorithmic bias appears on subgroup analysis (Obermeyer 31649194).

7_regulatory.note: REGULATORY NULL for the predictive fall-risk CDS itself: no product code/De Novo/510(k)/PMA exists. The adjacent HARDWARE landscape (all openFDA-verified): Class II Wearable Fall Injury Prevention Device SEC via De Novo DEN240021 (Active Protective Tango Belt); Class I fall alarm/sensor PJO/PJP and bed/patient monitors KMI/SBO (21 CFR 880.2400); cleared hardware K141877 (Leaf, KMI) and K233096 (PressureAlert, SBO). A novel predictive-CDS device would most plausibly pursue De Novo if it is a device at all. Continuously-updated-AI regulatory framing (van Amsterdam 42050181).

8_monitoring.note: Prospective monitoring for calibration drift (Davis 28379439/33157313), dataset/population shift (Finlayson 34260843), alert performance/alert-fatigue in the wild, and subgroup calibration. The Epic Sepsis Model external-validation failure (Wong 34152373) is the canonical cautionary example motivating mandatory surveillance; continuously-monitored-AI governance (van Amsterdam 42050181); ongoing subgroup-fairness monitoring per TRIPOD+AI (38626948).

expected_conclusions.regulatory_class: No FDA classification exists for a predictive EHR-based inpatient fall-RISK CDS algorithm. Existing fall-related FDA devices are HARDWARE: Class II wearable protective device (SEC, via De Novo DEN240021) and Class I alarms/sensors/monitors (PJO/PJP/KMI/SBO, all 21 CFR 880.2400). A novel predictive-CDS product would most plausibly pursue the De Novo route if determined to be a device, after a device/non-device CDS determination with regulatory counsel.
expected_conclusions.benchmark_precedent_only: No regulatory or consensus AUROC/sensitivity threshold exists that a fall-prediction model must meet. No comparator AUROC was retrieved from full text this session; any comparator figure is a study-defined placeholder. All operating thresholds, calibration slopes, sample sizes, and subgroup disparity limits are study-defined-placeholder values to be set by the study team.

coverage_gaps_expected: Drugs@FDA / drug labeling - not applicable: the system carries no drug/biologic product. Fall-risk-increasing drugs (sedatives, antihypertensives) enter only as model INPUTS, not as regulated products.

FDA AI/ML-enabled device list & CDS final-guidance documents - narrative documents, not openFDA API records; the ABSENCE of a fall-risk-prediction device was confirmable via openFDA but guidance-document text was not machine-verifiable this session.

Institutional / proprietary EHR-vendor embedded fall-risk models (vendor-native predictive scores) - not in any public database resolvable via the four permitted APIs; their performance claims could not be independently verified and were excluded.

Full-text effect sizes - PMIDs/DOIs resolved for identity only; no in-text numeric results extracted beyond registry enrollment counts, so any 'context' performance figure reflects abstract/summary level, not a full-text-verified estimate.
